response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and 15+ years of experience, they are fundamentally misaligned with this embedded networking security role. Most critically, they lack native-level Japanese language proficiency, which is an explicit required qualification. Additionally, their entire career has been in web development and application software, with zero embedded firmware, network security, or Linux kernel work experience. The only C programming exposure (6 months at National Instruments on a C IDE) falls far short of the strong UNIX C proficiency required for embedded switch firmware development.
strengths: Extensive cross-platform development experience with proven ability to quickly learn new technology domains and languages
Strong foundation in software craftsmanship, TDD practices, and CI/CD infrastructure that would support quality product development
Demonstrated leadership capability through organizing workshops, mentoring, and working effectively across distributed international teams
concerns: No native Japanese language proficiency despite being an explicit required qualification—a disqualifying gap for this Berlin-based team role
Career entirely in web and application development with zero embedded firmware, LAN-switch, or network security experience
Limited C programming background (6 months at NI); expertise is overwhelmingly JavaScript/TypeScript—insufficient for required strong UNIX C proficiency

key_factors.factor: Embedded firmware and network security domain experience
C programming proficiency for UNIX systems
